title: hemizonia parryi subsp. typica
intro: Hemizonia parryi subsp. typica, also known as Pary's tarplant, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to California and Baja California. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Hemizonia parryi subsp. typica can be prepared as an herbal infusion or tincture, often used topically to treat skin issues or taken internally to support overall health.
